21st-dev/1code ·GitHub ⭐ 5206 TypeScript · AI Coding Agent, Claude Code, Git Worktree, MCP, Desktop App, Developer Tools, Open Source

What it is

1Code is a powerful, open-source desktop client for running AI coding agents. It provides a visual interface for tools like Claude Code and Codex, featuring 'Cursor-like' diff previews, git worktree isolation for safe experimentation, and the ability to run agents in persistent cloud sandboxes while the user is offline.

Problems solved

  • Fragmented CLI interfaces for different AI coding agents
  • Risk of agents accidentally corrupting the main branch or local environment
  • Inability to monitor or continue long-running agent tasks when a laptop is closed

How to get started

Download the pre-built application or build from source using Bun. Once launched, connect your local repositories. You can start chat sessions that automatically create isolated git worktrees, allowing the AI to work on features or fixes without interrupting your main dev flow.

Detailed report

1. Positioning

21st-dev/1code is positioned as a 1code is a powerful, open-source desktop client for running ai coding agents. it provides a visual interface for tools like claude code and codex, featuring 'cursor-like' diff previews, git worktree isolation for safe experimentation, and the ability to run agents in persistent cloud sandboxes while the user is offline. It aims to provide developers with a robust, TypeScript-based solution for orchestration layer for coding agents (claude code, codex).

2. Pain Points & Advantages

Traditional methods in this domain often suffer from Fragmented CLI interfaces for different AI coding agents. 21st-dev/1code addresses this by 1code is a powerful, open-source desktop client for running ai coding agents. it provides a visual interface for tools like claude code and codex, featuring 'cursor-like' diff previews, git worktree isolation for safe experimentation, and the ability to run agents in persistent cloud sandboxes while the user is offline, offering a significant performance boost and better developer ergonomics compared to existing alternatives.

3. Technical Architecture

The project is built using TypeScript, adopting a modular and scalable design. It leverages modern industry standards to ensure compatibility across different environments, focusing on efficiency and ease of integration.

4. Core Implementation

Main logic revolves around orchestration layer for coding agents (claude code, codex). The implementation utilizes TypeScript's strengths to provide 1code is a powerful, open-source desktop client for running ai coding agents. it provides a visual interface for tools like claude code and codex, featuring 'cursor-like' diff previews, git worktree isolation for safe experimentation, and the ability to run agents in persistent cloud sandboxes while the user is offline, ensuring high reliability and performance under load.

5. Code Organization

The repository is structured logically, with core functionality separated from utilities and examples. This organization facilitates easy navigation and contribution for developers looking to extend the tool's capabilities.

6. Quick Start Suggestion

To get started, download the pre-built application or build from source using bun. once launched, connect your local repositories. you can start chat sessions that automatically create isolated git worktrees, allowing the ai to work on features or fixes without interrupting your main dev flow. This will allow you to quickly evaluate the tool and integrate it into your existing development workflow.

7. Summary

An essential, TypeScript-powered utility that streamlines orchestration layer for coding agents (claude code, codex); it effectively tackles Fragmented CLI interfaces for different AI coding agents while maintaining a high standard of code quality and usability.
